title: 10 possible side effects of Tripterygium wilfordii (leopard tree)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Tripterygium wilfordii causes fatigue due to its ability to suppress the immune system and alter the body's natural response to inflammation. The herb's bioactive compounds can cause a decrease in energy levels by modulating cytokine production and affecting the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, leading to feelings of tiredness and lethargy. Additionally, Tripterygium wilfordii's anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to its fatigue-inducing effects by reducing the body's natural defense mechanisms against infections and diseases.

Tripterygium wilfordii produces rash, which is often attributed to its allergenic properties. The plant's saponins and polyacetylenes can trigger an allergic reaction in some individuals, leading to the development of a rash. Additionally, Tripterygium wilfordii's ability to stimulate the immune system may also contribute to this side effect by increasing inflammation and irritating the skin.

Tripterygium wilfordii induces fever due to its immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The compound triptonide, present in the plant, has been shown to stimulate the production of cytokines, which are proteins that play a key role in the body's response to infection and inflammation, leading to an increase in body temperature. Additionally, the plant's ability to suppress prostaglandins, hormone-like substances involved in pain and fever regulation, may also contribute to its febrile effects.

Tripterygium wilfordii triggers diarrhea due to its ability to increase gastrointestinal motility and secretion. The herb's saponins and alkaloids are thought to stimulate the enteric nervous system, leading to increased contractions of the intestinal muscles and an accelerated movement of food through the digestive tract. This results in frequent bowel movements and a loose or watery stool, which can be uncomfortable and disrupt daily activities.

Tripterygium wilfordii increases sweating in some individuals. This side effect is likely due to the plant's ability to stimulate the sympathetic nervous system, leading to an increase in sweat gland activity and subsequent sweating. Additionally, Tripterygium wilfordii contains bioactive compounds that may affect the body's thermoregulation mechanisms, further contributing to this sweating phenomenon. As a result, users of Tripterygium wilfordii may experience increased perspiration as a common side effect.

Tripterygium wilfordii leads to vomiting due to its ability to irritate the stomach lining and cause inflammation. The compound tripterigium, present in the plant, can stimulate the secretion of gastric acid, leading to increased acidity and irritation of the mucous membranes. This can result in nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain in some individuals taking Tripterygium wilfordii supplements or extracts.

Tripterygium wilfordii disrupts menstrual cycle due to its ability to alter hormone levels in the body. The plant's compounds, such as tripterigoluminescin and sesquiterpenoids, have been shown to affect estrogen receptors, leading to changes in the balance of female hormones. This disruption can cause irregular periods, amenorrhea, or even complete cessation of menstruation. The exact mechanism is still unclear, but it is believed that Tripterygium wilfordii's hormonal effects are responsible for these menstrual cycle disruptions.

Tripterygium wilfordii affects vision due to its ability to alter the structure and function of retinal cells. Specifically, it can cause changes in visual acuity, peripheral vision, and color perception. Additionally, Tripterygium wilfordii has been shown to increase intraocular pressure, which can lead to glaucoma-like symptoms. These effects may occur due to the plant's ability to interfere with the normal functioning of retinal ganglion cells and optic nerve fibers.

Tripterygium wilfordii reduces appetite due to its ability to stimulate the production of cytokines, which can suppress appetite and increase satiety. This effect is thought to be mediated by the herb's active compounds, particularly tripterine and wilforlide A, which have been shown to modulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, leading to changes in appetite regulation. The reduction in appetite may also contribute to weight loss, a common reported side effect of Tripterygium wilfordii use.

Tripterygium wilfordii provokes insomnia in some individuals due to its ability to alter the body's sleep-wake cycle. The herb's active compounds, particularly triptolide and triptolidene, can bind to receptors in the brain, leading to increased alertness and difficulty falling asleep. This side effect is thought to be related to the herb's stimulating properties, which may interfere with normal sleep patterns. As a result, users of Tripterygium wilfordii may experience vivid dreams, restlessness, and fatigue as their body struggles to adapt to the altered sleep-wake cycle.

intro: Tripterygium wilfordii has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. These symptoms can occur due to the herb's ability to irritate the digestive tract and interfere with normal gut function. In severe cases, these issues can lead to malnutrition, dehydration, and even hospitalization, significantly impacting daily life and causing financial burdens. Additionally, Tripterygium wilfordii can also cause skin rashes, allergic reactions, and hormonal imbalances, further exacerbating the negative effects on a person's overall well-being.
intro_desc: Tripterygium wilfordii has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, dizziness, and skin rashes. These side effects can be caused by excessive dosage, interactions with other medications, or individual sensitivities. For instance, severe gastrointestinal problems can lead to dehydration and malnutrition, while skin rashes can cause social withdrawal and emotional distress. In some cases, these side effects can worsen people's lives, affecting their daily activities and overall well-being.
